description: 55M, HTN, CKD 3a (eGFR 52), LDL 145, ASCVD risk 8.5%. ACC/AHA primary prevention with KDIGO intensity modification to moderate. Demonstrates preemption + modification interaction: USPSTF Grade C preempted by ACC/AHA, ACC/AHA modified by KDIGO.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy (intensity modified by CKD)
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: adults 40-75 without diabetes, LDL 70-189, ASCVD risk ≥7.5% should initiate moderate-to-high-intensity statin. KDIGO modifies to moderate-intensity cap in CKD G3a (eGFR 52). ACC/AHA preempts USPSTF Grade C (which would also recommend statin but via shared decision-making). CKD is a cardiovascular risk enhancer that strengthens the case for statin initiation.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO
actions.rationale: KDIGO 1B: regular monitoring of eGFR and urine ACR for CKD staging and progression tracking. CKD G3a (eGFR 52) with normal albuminuria (ACR 18) indicates moderate risk. Monitor at least annually.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 140/86 mmHg is above target. HTN is both a CVD risk factor and an accelerator of CKD progression. Target BP <130/80 per KDIGO for CKD patients.
